title: F1-introspect: schema-introspection helper exposing Group A+B+C field metadata for panel rendering
description: Add a NEW typed helper module that exports `getPanelFields(): PanelFieldMeta[]` returning per-field metadata for the panel-editable fields (Group A + Group B + Group C). Each PanelFieldMeta carries `{ key, group, widget: 'select'|'text', label_i18n_key, current_value_extractor, default, enum_values?, validate, format_for_display }`. The helper is the single source of truth driving the clack panel's menu rendering — config.ts MUST NOT hardcode field lists. Reconcile the user-spec count discrepancy: schema currently has 7 hint-threshold fields in the user's enumerated list (`archive_hint_hours`, `archive_hint_cooldown_hours`, `underseed_node_threshold`, `review_hint_pending_count`, `review_hint_pending_age_days`, `maintenance_hint_days`, `maintenance_hint_cooldown_days`). The user spec said '8 — verify'. Resolve by also including `archive_edit_threshold` (also a Group B hint threshold per its docstring at fabric-config.ts:86) to reach the documented 8. If implementation finds this is wrong (e.g. archive_edit_threshold belongs to a different group), document the deviation in a code comment and proceed with 7.

files.change: Export `PanelFieldMeta` type and `getPanelFields()` function; export `getPanelFieldByKey(key: string)` accessor; re-export from packages/shared/src/index.ts so config.ts can import from `@fenglimg/fabric-shared`

implementation: Define `type PanelFieldGroup = 'A_locale' | 'B_hint_threshold' | 'C_audit'` (Group C currently = audit_mode per planning context)

Define `type PanelFieldMeta = { key: keyof FabricConfig; group: PanelFieldGroup; widget: 'select' | 'text'; label_i18n_key: string; default: string | number; enum_values?: readonly string[]; validate: (raw: string) => { ok: true, value: unknown } | { ok: false, error: string }; format_for_display: (v: unknown) => string }`
Group A (2 fields): `fabric_language` (select, 4 enum values from fabricLanguageSchema), `default_layer_filter` (select, 3 enum values from defaultLayerFilterSchema)
Group B (8 fields per user spec including archive_edit_threshold): each is positive-int text widget; validate via `z.coerce.number().int().positive().safeParse(raw)`
Group C: `audit_mode` (select, 3 enum values from auditModeSchema)
Each label_i18n_key follows the convention `cli.config.fields.<key>.label` and `cli.config.fields.<key>.description` — the strings themselves added in TASK-006 i18n keys
Add a code comment at the top documenting the 7-vs-8 reconciliation decision

reference.pattern: Existing zod schema introspection patterns in the shared package; existing barrel export style in packages/shared/src/index.ts
reference.examples: Use the zod schema's own `.shape` and `.options` introspection (e.g. fabricLanguageSchema.options gives the 4-string enum array) — DO NOT hardcode enum values

criteria: `getPanelFields()` returns at least 11 entries (2 Group A + 8 Group B + 1 Group C)
Each entry has all required PanelFieldMeta properties including a working `validate` fn
Group A enum values are derived from the schema (`fabricLanguageSchema.options`) NOT hardcoded — verified by deleting a value from the schema and observing the panel field disappear
Re-exported from `@fenglimg/fabric-shared` — `import { getPanelFields } from '@fenglimg/fabric-shared'` works in cli package
Behavioral expectation: adding a new field to fabricConfigSchema requires only adding one entry here for it to appear in the panel — no config.ts edit needed

rationale.chosen_approach: Schema introspection helper as separate module — keeps the schema file pure (Zod only), makes the panel-vs-non-panel distinction explicit, and provides a clean test surface for the metadata contract
alternatives_considered: Hardcode field list inside config.ts (rejected: violates 'single source of truth' principle, adding fields requires touching command code)
Use Zod's `.shape` reflection inline in config.ts (rejected: still requires hardcoding the 'is this in panel scope?' boolean per field)
Generate at build time from schema annotations (rejected: build complexity outweighs benefit at current scale)
decision_factors: Future Group D/E exposure (deferred per anti-scope) becomes a one-line addition to PanelFieldMeta list
Validation logic colocated with field metadata — easier to audit
Type-safe — `key: keyof FabricConfig` catches typos at compile time
rationale.tradeoffs: Adds ~150 LOC to shared package; offset by ~150 LOC removed from config.ts (no hardcoded list there)

manual_checks: Import getPanelFields() in a Node REPL, log entries, verify all expected keys + groups + widgets
Pass a known-bad input through one validate() to confirm error message is human-readable
success_metrics: 11 fields exposed (or 10 with comment if reconciliation lands at 7 Group B + 2 Group A + 1 Group C)
100% of validate() functions have at least one happy-path and one failure-path test
